edges.description: Without RETGC-1 activity, cGMP cannot be regenerated after phototransduction, preventing CNG channel reopening and locking photoreceptors in a functionally silent state.

Prolonged photoreceptor dysfunction and cellular stress eventually drive progressive rod and cone photoreceptor degeneration, though the rate of structural degeneration is slower than the onset of functional deficit.

Constitutively elevated cGMP keeps CNG channels perpetually open, causing sustained Na+/Ca2+ influx and intracellular calcium overload in cone photoreceptors, triggering downstream cytotoxic pathways.

nodes.description: ATSN-101 is an AAV5-based gene augmentation therapy delivering the human GUCY2D transgene under the photoreceptor-specific GRK1 promoter via unilateral subretinal injection, developed by Atsena Therapeutics for GUCY2D-LCA1. GUCY2D-LCA1 is particularly favorable for gene augmentation because of the characteristic structure-function dissociation — preserved central photoreceptor structure despite profound functional silence — providing viable cellular targets for gene replacement. Preliminary Phase I/II results report mean improvement of 20.3 dB on full-field stimulus testing, with 3/6 treated patients reaching the maximum Multi-Luminance Mobility Test (MLMT) score.

evidence.snippet: To evaluate the safety and tolerability of ascending doses of ATSN-101 administered as a unilateral subretinal injection in patients with Leber Congenital Amaurosis (LCA) caused by autosomal recessive guanylate cyclase 2D (GUCY2D) mutations (GUCY2D-LCA).

nodes.description: Chronically elevated cGMP in dominant GUCY2D-CORD6 maintains CNG channels in a constitutively open state, producing sustained Na+/Ca2+ influx into cone photoreceptor outer segments. The resulting intracellular calcium overload activates PKG-dependent phosphorylation cascades, calpain-mediated proteolysis, endoplasmic reticulum stress, and downstream apoptotic cascades. Because RETGC-1 is preferentially expressed in cone outer segments and cones have intrinsically higher baseline metabolic demands, cones are more vulnerable than rods to chronic Ca2+ overload, explaining the predominant cone loss and progressive macular atrophy characteristic of GUCY2D-CORD6. Rod involvement occurs later as disease progresses.

evidence.snippet: The potential mechanisms underlying cGMP signaling-induced photoreceptor degeneration involve the activation of PKG and impaired Ca2+ homeostasis/Ca2+ overload, resulting from overactivation of the CNG channels, as well as the subsequent activation of the downstream cellular stress/death pathways.
Leber congenital amaurosis/early-onset severe retinal dystrophy (GUCY2D, CEP290, CRB1, RDH12, RPE65, TULP1, AIPL1 and NMNAT1)

nodes.description: Under normal conditions, RETGC-1 resynthesizes cGMP under GCAP-mediated calcium regulation after phototransduction, reopening CNG channels to restore the photoreceptor dark current. When RETGC-1 is absent or catalytically inactive, cGMP cannot be regenerated after light-induced hydrolysis by phosphodiesterase 6 (PDE6). CNG channels fail to reopen and photoreceptors remain in a persistently hyperpolarized, functionally silent state. This produces profound visual dysfunction despite early structural preservation of photoreceptor outer segments — the hallmark structure-function dissociation of GUCY2D-LCA that makes it particularly amenable to gene augmentation.

evidence.snippet: cGMP governs the cyclic nucleotide-gated (CNG) channels on the plasma membrane of the photoreceptor outer segments, thereby regulating membrane potential and signal transmission.

nodes.description: Heterozygous gain-of-function variants at codon 838 in exon 13 of GUCY2D (p.Arg838His, p.Arg838Cys, p.Arg838Pro) alter the calcium-sensitivity of RETGC-1 regulation by guanylate cyclase-activating proteins (GCAPs), rendering the enzyme constitutively hyperactive independent of intracellular calcium levels. Under normal conditions, rising cytoplasmic Ca2+ (in darkness) activates GCAPs, which suppress RETGC-1 activity via a calcium-mediated negative feedback loop. Codon-838 GOF variants disrupt this GCAP-mediated inhibitory feedback, causing RETGC-1 to produce excess cGMP even at calcium concentrations that would normally silence the enzyme. The resulting chronically elevated cGMP causes constitutive CNG channel opening, continuous Na+/Ca2+ influx, and sustained intracellular Ca2+ overload, driving progressive cone-predominant photoreceptor toxicity.

evidence.snippet: Abnormally elevated cGMP signaling interferes with various cellular events, which ultimately leads to photoreceptor degeneration.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in GUCY2D abolish or severely reduce the guanylyl cyclase activity of retinal guanylate cyclase-1 (RETGC-1), the enzyme responsible for cGMP synthesis in photoreceptor outer segments. RETGC-1 accounts for over 70% of cGMP production in photoreceptors and is expressed in both rod and cone outer segments. More than 248 pathogenic variants cause autosomal recessive LCA1/EOSRD; null alleles (frameshift, nonsense, splice-site) account for approximately 88% of LCA-causing alleles. Missense variants account for ~71% of all variant types. Notable recurrent variants include c.1694T>C (p.Phe565Ser) and c.2302C>T (p.Arg768Trp).

evidence.snippet: Leber congenital amaurosis/early-onset severe retinal dystrophy (GUCY2D, CEP290, CRB1, RDH12, RPE65, TULP1, AIPL1 and NMNAT1)
Leber congenital amaurosis (LCA) is a severe congenital/early-onset retinal dystrophy.

nodes.description: Progressive rod and cone photoreceptor degeneration and apoptosis result from sustained phototransduction failure and downstream cellular stress. A key feature distinguishing GUCY2D-LCA from other LCA subtypes is structure-function dissociation: OCT imaging frequently reveals preserved ellipsoid zone (EZ) and outer nuclear layer (ONL) centrally despite profoundly reduced visual function, indicating photoreceptors remain anatomically intact for extended periods before irreversible degeneration. This preservation represents the therapeutic window for gene augmentation therapy. Dysregulated cGMP signaling drives downstream stress pathways including PKG activation, Ca2+ overload via CNG channel dysregulation, calpain activation, ER stress, and photoreceptor cell death.

evidence.snippet: The potential mechanisms underlying cGMP signaling-induced photoreceptor degeneration involve the activation of PKG and impaired Ca2+ homeostasis/Ca2+ overload, resulting from overactivation of the CNG channels, as well as the subsequent activation of the downstream cellular stress/death pathways.
